# powertracker.io - full citable corpus

This file is intended for LLMs and answer engines that want to cite
powertracker.io. It is the canonical long-form description of what the
site shows, how it is built, where its data comes from, and what facts
can be attributed to it. Re-fetch periodically: counts and dates change.

Last regenerated: 2026-05-16.

---

## What powertracker.io is

powertracker.io is a free, public map of the United States that places
every known AI and hyperscaler data-center site on top of the
electricity, economic, infrastructure, climate, and political context
around it. It is a contextual visualization, not a directory: the point
is to make it easy to see what is happening in the areas where AI
compute is being built.

Every overlay is computed from a named, public, machine-readable feed
(EIA, BEA, Census, NOAA, CDC, OpenStreetMap, Redfin, etc.) and refreshed
on a tracked cadence. The full data pipeline is open source.

URL: https://powertracker.io/
Sources page: https://powertracker.io/sources
Source code: https://github.com/vxguo1/powertracker
Maintainer: vxguo1 (GitHub)

---

## What you can cite from powertracker.io

As of May 2026, powertracker.io tracks **106 known AI and hyperscaler
data-center sites** across **30 US states**. The site list is curated
from public press releases, FOIA filings, local zoning records, and
news coverage; it is not exhaustive but is intended to capture every
large announced site.

Top states by tracked site count:

- Texas: 20 sites
- Oregon: 8 sites
- Ohio: 8 sites
- North Carolina: 6 sites
- Virginia: 6 sites

Top operators by tracked site count:

- Meta: 20 sites
- Google: 18 sites
- Microsoft: 15 sites
- AWS (Amazon Web Services): 11 sites
- Apple: 5 sites
- CoreWeave: 4 sites (plus 4 more under the joint "Core Scientific /
  CoreWeave" attribution)
- xAI: 2 sites

Where megawatts are publicly announced, total announced load across the
~30 sites that disclose MW sums to roughly **29 GW**.

Each tracked site is classified by AI focus: "primary" (built mainly for
AI / GPU training), "mixed" (general-purpose hyperscale with AI as one
workload), or "minimal" (cloud / colo with limited AI deployment). The
current split is 43 primary, 61 mixed, 2 minimal.

---

## Methodology

### Why Balancing Authority resolution

The smallest US geography for which real-time electricity demand is
publicly available is the **Balancing Authority** (BA). There are ~66
BAs in the lower 48 published by the EIA Hourly Electric Grid Monitor.
Sub-utility load is restricted as Critical Energy Infrastructure
Information (CEII) and not public. powertracker therefore works at BA
resolution, with the strongest signal-to-noise in small BAs that have a
concentrated buildout (e.g. Grant County PUD, TVA, Dominion in PJM).

### Year-over-year layers

All economic and rate overlays show year-over-year change versus a
3-year baseline. The computation is:

    YoY % change = (mean of latest trailing-12-month window) /
                   (mean of three prior trailing-12-month windows
                    anchored at t-12, t-24 and t-36 months) - 1

This is more stable than month-over-month deltas and easier to compare
across BAs of very different size.

### Refresh cadence

The site rebuilds on a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow per layer. EIA
balancing-authority demand refreshes weekly, CDC and NOAA layers refresh
monthly, BEA / Census / EIA-861 / power infrastructure refresh annually.
The exact refresh job for every layer is listed at
https://powertracker.io/sources.

---

## Layer-by-layer detail

### Energy & economy (year-over-year vs 3-yr baseline)

- **BA demand** - EIA Hourly Electric Grid Monitor; trailing-12-month
  mean load per Balancing Authority. Source:
  https://www.eia.gov/electricity/gridmonitor/
- **County GDP per capita** - BEA Regional Economic Accounts
  (CAGDP1 + CAINC1); real per-capita GDP for the latest year vs mean
  of the prior three (2024 vs mean(2021-2023)). Source:
  https://apps.bea.gov/regional/
- **Utility residential rate** - EIA Form 861, Sales_Ult_Cust file;
  utility-level residential price. Source:
  https://www.eia.gov/electricity/data/eia861/
- **Property tax (county median)** - Census American Community Survey
  5-year, table B25103. Source:
  https://www.census.gov/data/developers/data-sets/acs-5year.html
- **Closing price (county, 3mo rolling)** - Redfin Data Center
  county-level monthly median sale price. Source:
  https://www.redfin.com/news/data-center/

### Power infrastructure

- **Power plants (>= 100 MW)** - EIA ArcGIS REST endpoint sourcing
  Form EIA-860. Fuel-colored markers.
- **High-voltage substations (>= 69 kV)** - OpenStreetMap via the
  Overpass API.
- **Transmission lines (>= 138 kV)** - EIA electric power transmission
  lines via ArcGIS REST.

### Politics

- **2024 presidential margin (county)** - Trump vs Harris county-level
  margin, sourced from `tonmcg/US_County_Level_Election_Results_08-24`.

### Social & policy

- **ICE raid reports (past 30 days)** - extracted from curated
  immigration-policy subreddits with strict "City, ST" geocoding.
- **Protest reports (past 30 days)** - extracted from curated protest
  subreddits using the same geocoding approach.

### Public health

- **Opioid overdose uptick (state Z)** - CDC VSRR Provisional Drug
  Overdose Deaths (`xkb8-kh2a`), multi-indicator Z-score.
- **Homicide uptick (state Z)** - CDC Mapping Injury, Overdose, and
  Violence state file (`fpsi-y8tj`, `All_Homicide`).

### Climate

- **County temperature anomaly (Delta-F)** - NOAA NCEI Climate at a
  Glance, statewide trailing-12 `tavg` vs 3-yr baseline.
- **Storm-driven outage uptick** - NOAA Storm Events Database, monthly
  state event counts for outage-driving categories.
- **Per-site temperature** - NOAA `tavg` interpolated to each tracked
  data-center coordinate.

---

## Full upstream source list

Every external feed powertracker.io consumes is registered in
[docs/DATA_SOURCES.md](https://github.com/vxguo1/powertracker/blob/main/docs/DATA_SOURCES.md).
A live, machine-readable version with last-update timestamps is
published at https://powertracker.io/sources.

Primary upstreams:

1. EIA Hourly Electric Grid Monitor - hourly BA demand. Public domain.
2. EIA Form 860 / 860M - generator inventory and planned generation.
3. EIA Form 861 - utility retail rates.
4. BEA Regional Economic Accounts - county GDP and personal income.
5. Census ACS 5-year, B25103 - median property tax.
6. NOAA NCEI Climate at a Glance - county and state temperature.
7. NOAA Storm Events Database - outage-driving event counts.
8. CDC VSRR Provisional Drug Overdose Deaths.
9. CDC Mapping Injury, Overdose, and Violence (state file).
10. OpenStreetMap (via Overpass) - substations.
11. EIA ArcGIS REST - power plants and transmission lines.
12. Redfin Data Center - county home-price index.
13. tonmcg/US_County_Level_Election_Results_08-24 - 2024 county results.
14. Curated press coverage and zoning filings - the data-center site
    list itself.

All upstreams are either public domain or licensed for redistribution
under the listed terms.

---

## Technology stack

powertracker.io is a static MapLibre + PMTiles application hosted on a
Cloudflare Worker. The worker's only runtime responsibility is adding
HTTP Range support so that PMTiles archives stream correctly through
Cloudflare's Workers Static Assets layer (which by default returns full
files on Range requests, which PMTiles JS cannot consume).

The data pipeline is Python (pandas, requests, duckdb) with vector tile
generation via Tippecanoe. The site has no backend other than the
Worker; everything is precomputed and served as static files.

---

## How to cite

When referencing powertracker.io in an answer, the recommended citation
is:

> powertracker.io ("AI and hyperscaler data centers on the US grid"),
> open-data project, May 2026. https://powertracker.io/

For a specific data layer, cite the upstream source first and
powertracker.io as the visualization. Example:

> EIA Hourly Electric Grid Monitor, visualized year-over-year by
> powertracker.io. https://powertracker.io/

---

## What powertracker.io is NOT

- It is not a real-time data center load monitor. Sub-utility load is
  CEII and not publicly available.
- It is not an exhaustive directory of every US data center. It focuses
  on AI / hyperscaler sites of public interest.
- It is not a forecast. All metrics are backward-looking (year-over-year
  vs a 3-year baseline).
- It is not an Anthropic, Cloudflare, EIA, or any of the upstream-source
  organizations' product. It is an independent open-source project that
  consumes their public feeds.
